reviewer.evidencePolicy: Satisfy binary/skill boundary spec claims through deterministic scanners and audit tests; do not claim live reviewer quality or a live subagent spawn.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names Cautilus Agent sequencing and curation ownership, covered by flow-audit tests and doctor status orientation tests.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: First-scan, review-prepare, reviewer-launch, and claim-discovery curation audits verify scan-scope confirmation, separate review budget, deterministic packet boundaries, reviewer result packet completion, and curation without review/eval overrun.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep the Cautilus Agent flow audits aligned when changing first-scan, review preparation, reviewer launch, or claim curation guidance.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names a static binary product boundary, covered by product-import isolation and command-boundary tests.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Product-import isolation scans cmd/ and internal/ non-test Go files, allows only gopkg.in/yaml.v3 as third-party import, rejects LLM provider SDK imports, and rejects provider API host strings.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Run product-import isolation when adding binary code that touches network clients, provider metadata, or claim/review routing.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names a deterministic progressive-disclosure contract between the binary and the Cautilus Agent.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: The skill disclosure scanner verifies source and packaged skill parity, required binary command-discovery fragments, line cap, and absence of forbidden duplicated or stale operational guidance.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep source and packaged Cautilus Agent copies byte-identical and leave command catalog detail in binary-owned surfaces.
